question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer() does not configure a handshake timeout on the NettyServerBuilder, allowing the attacker to initiate a large number of half-open TLS handshakes that consume server resources without completing, preventing legitimate clients from establishing connections to verify Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shared applicationTaskExecutor in grpcServerConfigurer() combined with the reactive Flux pipeline in TopicMessageServiceImpl to cause a thread context propagation issue, where the wrong transaction context is associated with a database query, causing the query to return topic messages from a different database snapshot with different runningHash values?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the read-only TransactionTemplate from transactionOperationsReadOnly() does not configure a specific database isolation level, and on PostgreSQL the default READ COMMITTED isolation allows phantom reads, causing a subscriber to see a topic message appear and disappear between polls, creating a false impression of Hashgraph history manipulation?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of connection-level authentication in grpcServerConfigurer()'s NettyServerBuilder to impersonate a legitimate subscriber and inject a forged ConsensusTopicResponse into the response stream of another subscriber, causing that subscriber to receive a fabricated runningHash value as part of the Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a read-only transaction on the findByFilter() query path to cause a scenario where the EntityManager's second-level cache serves stale topic message data with outdated runningHash values, making the Hashgraph history appear to have been tampered with when it has not?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer() does not set a maximum header size on the NettyServerBuilder, allowing the attacker to send a ConsensusTopicQuery with extremely large metadata headers that cause memory pressure and trigger out-of-memory conditions that corrupt in-flight topic message data including runningHash bytes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that transactionOperationsReadOnly() creates a TransactionTemplate that is not scoped to a specific datasource, and in a multi-datasource environment the wrong datasource could be selected, causing the subscriber to receive topic messages and runningHash values from a replica that has not yet applied the latest Hashgraph history updates?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer() does not configure a maximum stream duration on the NettyServerBuilder, allowing the attacker to maintain an infinite subscription to a topic and continuously receive runningHash values, using timing analysis to detect when the hash chain is broken and identify tampered Hashgraph history entries?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the @Qualifier('readOnly') annotation on transactionOperationsReadOnly() could be shadowed by another bean with the same qualifier in a different configuration class, causing the wrong TransactionOperations implementation to be injected and bypassing the read-only constraint, allowing writes to topic message runningHash values?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer() does not configure a maximum number of connections per server on the NettyServerBuilder, allowing the attacker to open an unlimited number of connections that each consume maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection slots, effectively performing a resource exhaustion attack that prevents the Hashgraph history from being served?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that transactionOperationsReadOnly() does not configure a transaction name or description, making it impossible to distinguish read-only transactions from read-write transactions in database monitoring, allowing an attacker to hide write operations within what appears to be a read-only transaction that modifies topic message runningHash values?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer() uses a lambda to configure the NettyServerBuilder, and the lambda captures grpcProperties by reference, to cause a race condition if grpcProperties is modified concurrently, resulting in an inconsistent maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection value that allows more concurrent calls than intended and enables resource exhaustion attacks against the Hashgraph history service?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the read-only TransactionTemplate from transactionOperationsReadOnly() does not flush the EntityManager before executing queries, causing the subscriber to receive topic messages from the EntityManager's dirty cache that contain in-memory modifications to runningHash values that have not been persisted or validated?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer() does not configure a deadline or timeout for individual gRPC calls on the NettyServerBuilder, allowing the attacker to hold a subscribeTopic() call open indefinitely while slowly consuming topic messages, preventing the server from releasing resources and causing memory accumulation that eventually corrupts the in-memory representation of the Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that transactionOperationsReadOnly() does not set PROPAGATION_NOT_SUPPORTED or PROPAGATION_NEVER, allowing the read-only transaction to be nested within a read-write transaction, and thereby cause the subscriber to observe uncommitted topic message data including unvalidated runningHash values that could represent tampered Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer() does not configure a maximum number of streams per connection on the NettyServerBuilder beyond maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection, allowing the attacker to multiplex many streams over HTTP/2 and bypass the intended concurrency limit, enabling a larger-scale resource exhaustion attack against the Hashgraph history streaming service?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the TransactionTemplate created by transactionOperationsReadOnly() does not configure a specific connection timeout, causing the transaction to wait indefinitely for a database connection, and during this wait period the subscriber receives no topic messages, creating an artificial gap in the Hashgraph history that could be mistaken for tampering?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer() does not configure a maximum inbound message size on the NettyServerBuilder, allowing the attacker to send a ConsensusTopicQuery with an extremely large topicID or timestamp value that causes integer overflow in the EntityId.of() conversion, resulting in the subscriber receiving topic messages from a completely different topic with a different runningHash chain?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that transactionOperationsReadOnly() creates a singleton TransactionTemplate bean that is shared across all threads, and if the underlying PlatformTransactionManager is not thread-safe, cause a race condition that corrupts the transaction state and allows a write operation to execute within the read-only transaction, modifying topic message runningHash values?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer() does not configure a maximum number of pending connections on the NettyServerBuilder, allowing the attacker to fill the TCP accept queue with half-open connections, causing legitimate clients to be unable to connect and verify the integrity of the Hashgraph history through the runningHash chain?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the read-only TransactionTemplate from transactionOperationsReadOnly() does not configure a specific fetch size for database queries, causing the JPA provider to use a default fetch size that loads all topic messages into memory at once, enabling a memory exhaustion attack that corrupts the JVM heap and causes incorrect runningHash values to be served?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer() configures the NettyServerBuilder with a shared applicationTaskExecutor that has no rejection policy configured, causing new tasks to be silently dropped when the executor is full, resulting in topic message delivery gaps that make the Hashgraph history appear to have been tampered with?
